name: The Accessible Grounds Café
description: A fully accessible café with step-free access, braille menus, and a quiet corner for sensory-sensitive visitors. Known for its inclusive atmosphere and welcoming staff.

reviews.comment: Brilliant café with excellent wheelchair access. The ramp is well-maintained and the staff are incredibly helpful.
Good braille menu available. The quiet zone is a great touch for those of us who need a calmer environment.
As a wheelchair user, this is my go-to café. Everything is at a reachable height and the aisles are wide.

name: Central Public Library
description: A modern library with full accessibility features including elevator access to all floors, large print collections, and assistive technology stations.

reviews.comment: The best accessible library I've visited. Screen readers at every station and staff trained in accessibility.
Excellent elevator access and the automatic doors make entry effortless with my mobility scooter.

name: Riverside Cinema
description: An inclusive cinema experience with wheelchair-accessible seating, audio description headsets, and subtitled screenings throughout the week.

reviews.comment: Love the sensory-friendly screenings! Lower volume and softer lighting make all the difference for my autistic son.
Audio description headsets work perfectly. Finally a cinema that caters properly to visually impaired visitors.

name: Green Park Fitness Centre
description: A fitness centre with adapted equipment, pool hoist, and trained accessibility staff. Offers inclusive fitness classes for all ability levels.

reviews.comment: Great adapted equipment and the pool hoist is easy to use independently. Staff are well-trained.
The inclusive fitness classes are wonderful. I feel welcome and the instructors adapt exercises brilliantly.

name: Heritage Museum
description: A museum committed to accessibility with tactile exhibits, BSL guided tours, and step-free access throughout all galleries.

reviews.comment: The tactile exhibits are fantastic. Being able to touch replicas of artefacts makes the museum come alive.
BSL tours are excellent. Really appreciate the museum investing in deaf accessibility.

name: Harbour View Restaurant
description: A waterfront restaurant with full step-free access, allergy-aware menus in multiple formats, and trained accessibility champions on every shift.

reviews.comment: Lovely food and the braille menu is a nice touch. Would love to see a hearing loop installed too.
Good wheelchair access and the lowered counter makes ordering much easier. Staff are attentive.
